title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Injecting malicious JavaScript into web pages viewed by other users.
fullExplanation: XSS occurs when an attacker injects malicious scripts into content from otherwise trusted websites. The victim's browser executes the malicious script, which can steal cookies, session tokens, or other sensitive information. There are three main types: Reflected XSS (script in URL), Stored XSS (script saved in database), and DOM-based XSS (client-side script manipulation).

types.name: Reflected XSS
types.description: Malicious script is part of the request (URL parameter). Server reflects it back in response.

types.name: Stored XSS
types.description: Malicious script is stored in database (comment, profile). Executed when page loads.

types.name: DOM-based XSS
types.description: Vulnerability in client-side JavaScript that processes user input unsafely.

attackSteps.description: Search for input fields, URL parameters, or user-generated content
Try payloads like or
Send malicious link to victim (reflected) or post comment (stored)
Victim's browser executes script, sends cookies/tokens to attacker

keyFacts: XSS is one of the most common web vulnerabilities
Can steal session cookies and hijack accounts
HttpOnly flag prevents JavaScript from accessing cookies
Content Security Policy (CSP) blocks inline scripts
Always encode/escape user input before displaying
DOM-based XSS happens entirely in the browser

defenses.name: Input Validation
defenses.description: Whitelist allowed characters, reject suspicious input

defenses.name: Output Encoding
defenses.description: HTML-encode all user input before displaying: < becomes <

defenses.name: Content Security Policy
defenses.description: HTTP header that blocks inline scripts and restricts script sources

defenses.name: HttpOnly Cookies
defenses.description: Set HttpOnly flag so JavaScript cannot access cookies

defenses.name: X-XSS-Protection Header
defenses.description: Browser feature to detect and block reflected XSS
